Let's get funky. Alright, so reselling ascension, the last of the modules, let's get funky. Alright, so the resell portion is after you receive money and then you did the fulfillment, which is that arrow, we are now at the mo money section. So here's the overview. There are two sales opportunities in the resell portion of a front-end program. So the first is what we call the abandoned hopes, which is at the halfway point, which is week end, their second week, and during that third week before, like, basically, you were going to be scheduling them for the follow week to come in. So they should have had three way in, so they should be halfway through the program when they meet with you. So you should have the stat of that way in so that you can use that to extrapolate whether they'll be successful, etc. Alright, so there are the offer for the abandoned hopesale is kind of an irresistible offer, that's the stack that we're going for here, and what you want to offer here is whatever play you did not use, so if you used the detox, for example, you could use any other play in the arsenal, but if you wanted to pick the six-week challenge one or the 42-day transformation, whatever, you can use that as a hook and a stick on top of signing up. So you're going to use that as one of the pieces of the irresistible offer, and you're going to use that as like, this is the next step, so it's kind of been assumed like, this is the next thing you need to plead, okay? So I hope I'm clear about that. If someone did six weeks, it's like, cool, you'd fit, we're gonna be, you know, recalibrating, we want to make sure that we increase your metabolism, recalibrate you, which will set you up for longer term success and weight loss. The worst thing you can do is stop now because then you'll actually be more prone to gaining the weight back, which is not what you want, right? Of course not. Okay, the next piece of this play is you're going to be offering them an automatic way, okay? So what we mean by that is you just say, listen, one of the next videos, you'll be offering them the opportunity to actually what you have to offer them if they can all of a sudden be risk-free and still get the challenge free. I mean, they're already getting it free because there's no risk, but like this is effectively giving them that time period for nothing and they're taking that money and crediting it forward, which all then increases the perceived value of what they're getting, all right? So that is the abandoned hope sale in a nutshell. I'll explain the what to say and when to say it in the actual sales video, but this is the overarching explanation of how the abandoned hope sale works and the first opportunity. The second opportunity is the backend, right? So if you have not succeeded to sell them the abandoned hope sale, then you have to sell them on the back. We're going to close 70 to 80% of the sales, okay? So you should be shooting for 70 to 80% of people who signed up for the challenge to be closed at the halfway point. So you need to very much approach that as a sale, all right? The backend is only to mop up anyone who you couldn't get a strong commitment from at the halfway point and then you can close them out at the end, all right? So at the end, we have kind of two scenarios. We have one in lost. If someone won, the goal is to get them to credit that forward and obviously not refund. That's the goal. We can also go for the paid and full, but we'll start there and then work our way down. The other alternative is if someone lost, the goal is to get them onto EFT. So both scenarios are the same except we're really, really, really trying to get these people not to refund their money. It's not kind of worst case scenarios so that you can still not have to worry about it. With the lost scenario, these people are some of the highest value clients out there. Ironically, you wouldn't think that, but people who are success, success are actually the most likely people to stay on recurring. I think it's because they see results, but they didn't in the goal. So there's some sort of open loop there. But those people, all of your effort has to be servicing the crap out of them from week three to the end if they did not commit. Because if those people come in at week six, they'll sign up. So I'm telling you, if you get them to the way out, they'll sign up. You actually give to them, which is a money, they get paid per pound. So they're still actually salvaging a decent amount of their initial deposit towards the membership. So they don't feel like they're not getting a great deal. So we're still going to make them feel like they're getting an awesome deal no matter what. We're still applauding them on their success and we're kind of framing it relative to the long term, which is like whether you lost 18 pounds or 22, it doesn't change in a one year period. And so it's just, it's the habits that matter. That's kind of what we remind them of. The last part of this with the money stuff is I will explain how the terms of how long they want to stay to get to work your way back to whatever number you want them to be at for the membership. So I hope that makes sense. And that is the basic overview of how this works. And let's get to sales opportunity number one. You better know.
